item.title: Macromedia mysteries
item.description: This summer, I will be helping bring the Macromedia Director engine to maturity as my project for Google Summer of Code 2020. Tonight I found myself quite deep in a major rewrite of the core rendering pipeline, and when I got tired of that I decided to finally learn how to use Hugo. That’s why you are reading this now.

item.title: Trivia
item.description: Hello, fellow surfer! I’m a mathematics major at Liberty University. I also delude myself into thinking that I have time for my minors in philosophy, computer science, and piano performance. I was born and raised in Hawaii, and I now live in the eastern United States. When I was ten, I first started programming with QBasic. Quickly thereafter, I switched to Visual Basic and C. After a long hiatus for music and competitive forensics, I learned C++ at university and began the arduous trek toward Emacs mastery.
